final_response:
I apologize, but I'm unable to run bash commands in this environment due to permission restrictions. However, I can tell you that you can typically find your UID (User ID) and GID (Group ID) by running these commands in a terminal:

```bash
id
```

This will display output like:
```
uid=1000(username) gid=1000(groupname) groups=1000(groupname),999(docker),...
```

Or you can use specific commands:
```bash
id -u    # Shows just the UID
id -g    # Shows just the GID
```
